_README: Committed B6 derived-row enumeration (X0c; plan §1.1 B6). Produced by the x0c-derived-rows workflow (run wf_f8a442df: one miner per ledgered folder parsing live_hypotheses incl. T2-bound refuted targets, one doc-miner per ledgerless folder, consolidation with S2-dedupe + plan coverage check) + one minted coverage-gap row (drv_sad_wc3). Merged into the manifest by meta/build_v2_manifest.py as corpus 'derived'. Statements are formalization-grade snapshots of the attack folders at ledger_commit; T-track milestones re-snapshot at start (plan §5).

parked.reason: proof-strategy schema (swap repairs strict-exit or decreases a potential) without a pinned single proposition; revisit if the attack pins one
non-round-residue peel/reinsert invariant has no candidate proposition; open crux covered indirectly by drv_heroforest_h6_halfa + drv_heroforest_h7

rows.title: Antichain Coverage Conjecture (refuted at n=12)
rows.statement: Definitions as in the Universal Replacement Conjecture row (2-pole side (H,(r1,r2)), port states, boundary trace, Trace(H,(r1,r2))). Consider the finite family L10 = {Trace(H',(s1,s2)) : (H',(s1,s2)) a 2-pole side with |V(H')| ≤ 10} (the n≤10 gadget lattice; 59 distinct trace sets), and let its ⊆-minimal elements be the 7 minimal trace classes (class ids C0, C2, C3, C4, C5, C6, C7; minimum realising orders 4, 10, 10, 10, 6, 8, 8; a mutually ⊆-incomparable antichain whose union is the full 16-trace universe; archived in data/gadget_lattice_2pole_n10_both.json). REFUTED CLAIM: for every 2-pole side (H,(r1,r2)) with |V(H)| ≥ 4, at least one of these 7 minimal trace sets is a subset of Trace(H,(r1,r2)). (Equivalently, in the folder's wording: every realisable 2-pole trace set contains a minimal lattice trace set — which would have made every side of order > 10 trace-replaceable by a minimal-class gadget and closed Lemma 2 from the n≤10 lattice alone.)

rows.title: Core Absorption Lemma (all-orders form)
rows.statement: Definitions as in the Universal Replacement Conjecture row (2-pole side, port states, Trace, compatibility-universal), plus: a 3-decomposition of a connected cubic (3-regular simple) graph G is a partition E(G)=T⊔C⊔M with T a spanning tree, C a 2-regular subgraph (every vertex of C has degree exactly 2 in C; possibly empty), M a matching (possibly empty); a 2-edge-cut F={e1,e2} of G is essential iff the four endpoints of e1,e2 are pairwise distinct; its sides are the two components of G−F, each a 2-pole side whose ports are the two cut-edge endpoints it contains. CONJECTURE. Let G be a connected cubic simple graph with no 3-decomposition and with |V(G)| minimum among such graphs (a smallest counterexample), let F={e1,e2} be an essential 2-edge-cut of G, and let H be a side of F with ports (a1,a2). Then at least one of: (a) H contains a bridge that is also a bridge of G; (b) H contains a bridge e* such that {e1,e*} or {e2,e*} is an essential 2-edge-cut of G whose smaller side has strictly fewer vertices than the smaller side of F; (c) Trace(H,(a1,a2)) contains, as a subset, at least one of the ten explicit core trace sets C0, C2, C3, C4, C5, C6, C7, C8, C22, C23 of the n≤12 gadget lattice (each a concrete finite trace set realised by a specific 2-pole side on at most 12 vertices, archived in data/gadget_lattice_2pole_n12_both.json and data/n14_absorbing_core.json; e.g. C0 = the diamond K4-minus-edge set {(T_CC,T_T) split, (T_T,T_CC) split, (T_TM,T_TM) joined}); (d) Trace(H,(a1,a2)) is compatibility-universal.

rows.title: Lemma C — essentially-3-connected C0/compatibility dichotomy
rows.statement: Definitions as in the Universal Replacement Conjecture row: 2-pole side (H,(r1,r2)) (connected simple max-degree-3 graph, exactly two degree-2 ports r1 ≠ r2, all other vertices degree 3); port states T_T=(T;{T,T}), T_TM=(T;{T,M}), M_TT=(M;{T,T}), T_CC=(T;{C,C}), C_TC=(C;{T,C}); boundary trace (χ,π) and Trace(H,(r1,r2)) defined via partitions E(H)=T_H⊔C_H⊔M_H with C_H a disjoint union of cycles, M_H a matching, T_H acyclic, side-edge colours at each port matching χ, every component of (V(H),T_H) containing a boundary-T port, and π the partition of boundary-T ports by T_H-component; compatibility-universal = Trace contains all four axis types (TT-joined, TT-split, (T,M), (M,T)). Call (H,(r1,r2)) essentially 3-connected iff H has no bridge and no cut vertex, and every 2-element vertex set whose removal disconnects H equals the neighbourhood N(r1) or N(r2) of a port. Let Trace(C0) denote the 3-element trace set of the diamond gadget K4-minus-an-edge (graph6 'C^', ports its two degree-2 vertices): {(T_CC,T_T) with π={{r1},{r2}}, (T_T,T_CC) with π={{r1},{r2}}, (T_TM,T_TM) with π={{r1,r2}}}. CONJECTURE. For every essentially-3-connected 2-pole side (H,(r1,r2)) with |V(H)| ≥ 4, either Trace(C0) ⊆ Trace(H,(r1,r2)), or Trace(H,(r1,r2)) is compatibility-universal.

rows.title: Cycle-bypass lemma (Lemma C piece 1)
rows.statement: With the same definitions as the Lemma C row (2-pole side, port states, Trace, essentially 3-connected): for every essentially-3-connected 2-pole side (H,(r1,r2)) with |V(H)| ≥ 4, both bypass traces are realisable, i.e. (T_CC,T_T) with π={{r1},{r2}} ∈ Trace(H,(r1,r2)) and (T_T,T_CC) with π={{r1},{r2}} ∈ Trace(H,(r1,r2)). Concretely, for each i ∈ {1,2} there is a partition E(H)=T_H⊔C_H⊔M_H in which C_H is a vertex-disjoint union of cycles one of which passes through r_i using both of r_i's H-edges, the other port has both its H-edges in T_H, M_H is a matching, T_H is acyclic, and every component of (V(H),T_H) contains a boundary-T port (r_i forming its own singleton T-component).

rows.title: Matching-joined / M-axis dichotomy (Refined Lemma C, piece 2)
rows.statement: With the same definitions as the Lemma C row (2-pole side, port states, Trace, essentially 3-connected): for every essentially-3-connected 2-pole side (H,(r1,r2)) with |V(H)| ≥ 4, either (T_TM,T_TM) with π={{r1,r2}} ∈ Trace(H,(r1,r2)) (both ports in the matched state T_TM with T_H connecting the two ports in one tree component), or both M-axis traces are realisable: (M_TT,T_T) with π={{r2}} ∈ Trace(H,(r1,r2)) and (T_T,M_TT) with π={{r1}} ∈ Trace(H,(r1,r2)).

rows.title: Target theorem — minimal counterexample is 3-edge-connected
rows.statement: A 3-decomposition of a connected cubic (3-regular simple) graph G is a partition E(G) = T ⊔ C ⊔ M where T is a spanning tree of G, C is a 2-regular subgraph (every vertex of C has degree exactly 2 in C; possibly empty), and M is a matching (possibly empty). CONJECTURE (target theorem of the attack). Every connected cubic simple graph G that admits no 3-decomposition and has minimum |V(G)| among all connected cubic simple graphs admitting no 3-decomposition is 3-edge-connected (no set of at most 2 edges disconnects G). (This implies the non-vacuous reduction: if any connected cubic graph lacks a 3-decomposition, then some 3-edge-connected one does.)

rows.title: Sub-lemma 1' (subcubic existence at a bridge port)
rows.statement: Let H be a connected simple graph of maximum degree 3 with exactly one vertex r of degree 2, every other vertex having degree exactly 3 (a 1-pole side whose unique port r has port-degree 1). Then there exists a partition E(H) = T_H ⊔ C_H ⊔ M_H such that: T_H is a spanning tree of H; every vertex incident to an edge of C_H has exactly two C_H-edges (C_H is a vertex-disjoint union of cycles, possibly empty); M_H is a matching (possibly empty); and at r either (port state T_T) both H-edges at r are in T_H, i.e. deg_{T_H}(r)=2 and deg_{C_H}(r)=deg_{M_H}(r)=0, or (port state T_TM) one H-edge at r is in T_H and the other is in M_H, i.e. deg_{T_H}(r)=1, deg_{M_H}(r)=1, deg_{C_H}(r)=0.

rows.title: Simple-swap universality hypothesis (refuted at n=14)
rows.statement: Definitions as in the Lemma C row (2-pole side, port states, Trace, essentially 3-connected; Trace(C0) = the diamond trace set). A (T_TM,T_TM)-split partition of a 2-pole side (H,(r1,r2)) is a partition E(H)=T_H⊔C_H⊔M_H realising the trace (T_TM,T_TM) with π={{r1},{r2}}: each port has one inside T_H-edge and one inside M_H-edge (boundary colour T), C_H is a disjoint union of cycles, M_H a matching, and T_H has exactly two components, A containing r1 and B containing r2. For i∈{1,2} let w_i be the M_H-neighbour of r_i. REFUTED CLAIM (working hypothesis behind the simple-swap route to the M-axis dichotomy): every one of the 7 unoriented essentially-3-connected 2-pole sides at |V(H)|=14 with Trace(C0) ⊄ Trace(H) (the n=14 C0-exception graphs) admits at least one (T_TM,T_TM)-split partition in which simultaneously w_1 ∈ B and w_2 ∈ A — the configuration under which the simple double edge-swap (recolour each port's inside M-edge to T and its boundary half-edge to M) yields realisations of both M-axis traces (M_TT,T_T) and (T_T,M_TT) without creating a cycle in T_H.

rows.title: Universal Replacement Conjecture
rows.statement: Definitions. A 2-pole side is a pair (H,(r1,r2)) where H is a connected simple graph of maximum degree 3 with exactly two vertices r1 ≠ r2 of degree 2 (the ports), all other vertices of degree exactly 3 (this is exactly the form of a side of an essential 2-edge-cut of a cubic graph). The five port states are pairs (boundary colour; multiset of colours of the port's two H-edges): T_T=(T;{T,T}), T_TM=(T;{T,M}), M_TT=(M;{T,T}), T_CC=(T;{C,C}), C_TC=(C;{T,C}). A boundary trace is a pair (χ,π) where χ assigns a port state to each port and π is a partition of the set of ports whose χ-boundary colour is T. (χ,π) ∈ Trace(H,(r1,r2)) iff there is a partition E(H) = T_H ⊔ C_H ⊔ M_H with: (i) every vertex incident to C_H has exactly two C_H-edges (C_H a vertex-disjoint union of cycles); (ii) M_H a matching; (iii) T_H acyclic; (iv) at each port the multiset of colours of its two H-edges equals the side-colour multiset of its χ-state; (v) every connected component of the spanning subgraph (V(H),T_H) (isolated vertices allowed) contains at least one port of χ-boundary colour T; (vi) two boundary-T ports are in the same block of π iff they are in the same component of (V(H),T_H). Trace(H,(r1,r2)) is compatibility-universal iff it contains traces of all four axis types: both boundary colours T with π={{r1,r2}} (joined); both boundary colours T with π={{r1},{r2}} (split); boundary colours (T,M); boundary colours (M,T). (By the folder's proved Theorem 3.14 this is equivalent to: every trace τ in the 16-element universe U of realisable 2-pole traces is boundary-compatible with some σ ∈ Trace(H,(r1,r2)).) CONJECTURE. For every 2-pole side (H,(r1,r2)) with |V(H)| ≥ 12, at least one of: (a) there exists a 2-pole side (H0,(s1,s2)) with |V(H0)| < |V(H)| and Trace(H0,(s1,s2)) ⊆ Trace(H,(r1,r2)) (H is trace-contained by a strictly smaller gadget class, hence Lemma-2-replaceable); or (b) Trace(H,(r1,r2)) is compatibility-universal.
rows.notes: Plan coverage target 'Universal Replacement Conjecture' — present.

rows.title: B1: 3-connected + diam >= 4 implies D2 >= n (collisions <= surplus)
rows.statement: For every finite 3-connected simple graph G with diam(G) >= 4: D2(G) >= |V(G)|, where D2(G) := number of distinct lines line(a,b) over pairs a,b with d(a,b) = 2 (lines as in row H5). Equivalently, with G2 the distance-2 graph on V(G) (u ~ v iff d_G(u,v) = 2): collisions <= surplus, where surplus := |E(G2)| - |V(G)| and collisions := |E(G2)| - D2(G).

rows.title: B1 localization G3 (refuted): 2*collisions <= E(DE u N(DE))
rows.statement: For every finite 3-connected simple graph G with diam(G) >= 4: 2*collisions(G) <= E(DEN), where (lines as in row H5) G2 is the graph on V(G) with u ~ v iff d_G(u,v) = 2; degG2(v) := #{u : d_G(u,v) = 2}; D2(G) := number of distinct lines line(a,b) over pairs with d(a,b) = 2; collisions(G) := |E(G2)| - D2(G); DE := {v : ecc(v) = diam(G)}; DEN := DE u N_G(DE) (closed G-neighbourhood union of the diameter-eccentricity set); and E(S) := sum over v in S of (degG2(v) - 2).

rows.title: B2: 2-separable + diam >= 4 + D2 < n implies BIGTORSO >= n
rows.statement: Let G be a finite 2-connected simple graph with diam(G) >= 4 that has a 2-vertex cut (is not 3-connected) and satisfies D2(G) < |V(G)| (D2 as in row B1). Then BIGTORSO(G) >= |V(G)|. Definition: for every pair {a,b} whose removal disconnects G and every connected component C of G - {a,b}, the torso is the edge-weighted graph on C u {a,b} consisting of all edges of G inside C u {a,b} with weight 1, plus, if ab is not an edge of G, a virtual edge ab whose weight equals the distance from a to b in the induced subgraph of G on V(G) \ C (which contains a and b). Lines of the torso are defined by the same betweenness rule applied to the torso's weighted shortest-path metric, and ell(torso) counts its distinct lines; BIGTORSO(G) := max of ell(torso) over all such 2-cuts {a,b} and components C.

rows.title: Lemma A easy-branch residual: D' >= excess for marked 2-connected graphs
rows.statement: Let B be a finite 2-connected simple graph, u in V(B) a marked vertex, S := V(B) \ {u}, d the shortest-path metric of B. Write x <= y iff d(u,x) + d(x,y) = d(u,y). For s in S set Sigma_s := {x in S : x <= s or s <= x}, F_s := {x in S : d(x,u) + d(u,s) = d(x,s)}, and the apex trace A_s := Sigma_s u F_s. Partition S into fibers by the value of Sigma_s; in each fiber designate as representative the member of minimum depth d(u,.), ties broken by smallest vertex index; the remaining members are non-representatives. Put a := #{A_s : s a non-representative}, excess := (#non-representatives) - a, and D' := #{ line_B(x,y) : x != y in V(B), u not in line_B(x,y), line_B(x,y) != S } (the u-avoiding proper lines of B, lines as in row H5). Claim: D' >= excess.
rows.notes: This row carries the plan's 'C2' Chen-Chvatal coverage target (oracle handles scripts/c2_excess_hall_gate.py, scripts/c2_four_prime_review_gate.py).

rows.title: Non-adjacent-pair line bound (refuted): F_far >= n for n >= 9
rows.statement: For every finite connected simple graph G with no vertex of degree 1, diam(G) >= 4 and |V(G)| >= 9: F_far(G) := #{ line(a,b) : a,b in V(G), d(a,b) >= 2 } >= |V(G)| (lines as in row H5).

rows.title: R-mirror Hall (refuted): proper-R-trace lines >= |R|
rows.statement: In the Lemma A setting of row H10 (G finite connected simple, no vertex of degree 1, diam(G) >= 4, not 2-connected; B a leaf block with unique cut vertex u, S := V(B) \ {u}, R := G - S), every leaf block satisfies Q_rmirror := #{ lines L of G : the empty set is a proper subset of L intersect V(R), which is a proper subset of V(R) } >= |V(R)| (lines as in row H5).

rows.title: Spine injectivity (refuted): some diametral geodesic has pairwise-distinct spine lines
rows.statement: Every finite connected simple graph G with no vertex of degree 1 and d := diam(G) >= 4 has a geodesic v_0, v_1, ..., v_d (consecutive vertices adjacent and d(v_0, v_i) = i for all i) such that the d lines line(v_0, v_i), 1 <= i <= d, are pairwise distinct (lines as in row H5).

rows.title: Margin-floor monotonicity (refuted): M(n) non-decreasing
rows.statement: The function M(n) := min{ ell(G) + br(G) - n : G a connected simple graph on n vertices with no vertex of degree 1 and diam(G) >= 4 } is non-decreasing in n for n >= 8 (ell, br, lines as in row H1).

rows.title: F_0 is complete: no pendant-free bad graph of order >= 9
rows.statement: For every finite connected simple graph G with |V(G)| >= 9 and no vertex of degree 1 (pendant-free), ell(G) + br(G) >= |V(G)|. Definitions: d is the shortest-path metric of G; [uvw] :<=> d(u,v)+d(v,w)=d(u,w); for a != b, line(a,b) := {a,b} u {x in V(G) : [abx] or [axb] or [xab]}; ell(G) := number of distinct sets line(a,b) over unordered pairs a != b; br(G) := number of bridges (cut edges) of G. Combined with the exhaustively verified census for |V| <= 8 (exactly 12 pendant-free graphs with ell+br < |V|, at orders 4,5,6,8), this is equivalent to: the set of bad graphs (connected, pendant-free, ell+br < |V|) equals the explicit 12-graph family F_0 of arXiv:1606.06011 Figs 1-3.
rows.notes: Strictly stronger than parent Conjecture 2.2 (pins F_0 + order bound) — verified not an s2-duplicate.

rows.title: Lemma A: leaf-block amalgam inequality (non-2-connected case of H5)
rows.statement: Let G be a finite connected simple graph with no vertex of degree 1, diam(G) >= 4, and at least one cut vertex (not 2-connected). Let B be any leaf block of G (a maximal 2-connected subgraph containing exactly one cut vertex u of G; pendant-freeness forces every leaf block to be a non-bridge block with |V(B)| >= 3), S := V(B) \ {u}, and R := G - S (the induced subgraph on V(G) \ S; it is connected and contains u). Then ell(G) >= |S| + max(ell(R), |R|), where lines/ell are as in row H5 and ell(R) is computed in R with R's own shortest-path metric. This inequality implies ell(G) >= |V(G)| for every non-2-connected H5 input (the max term self-compensates any line-deficit of R; no induction on R needed).
rows.notes: Plan coverage target 'Lemma A' — present.

rows.title: Lemma B: 2-connected + diam >= 4 implies ell >= n
rows.statement: For every finite 2-connected simple graph G (|V(G)| >= 3, connected, no cut vertex) with diam(G) >= 4: ell(G) >= |V(G)|, with lines/ell defined as in row H5 (line(a,b) := {a,b} u {x : d(a,x)+d(x,b)=d(a,b) or d(a,b)+d(b,x)=d(a,x) or d(x,a)+d(a,b)=d(x,b)}; ell = number of distinct lines).

rows.title: Bounded deficit: n - (ell+br) uniformly bounded on pendant-free graphs
rows.statement: There exists a constant C >= 0 such that every finite connected simple graph G with no vertex of degree 1 satisfies |V(G)| - (ell(G) + br(G)) <= C, where d is the shortest-path metric, [uvw] :<=> d(u,v)+d(v,w)=d(u,w), line(a,b) := {a,b} u {x : [abx] or [axb] or [xab]} for a != b, ell(G) := number of distinct lines, br(G) := number of bridges. (Empirical candidate C = 3, the deficit of C4, the maximum over all 12 known bad graphs.)

rows.title: Every pendant-free bad graph is bridgeless
rows.statement: For every finite connected simple graph G with no vertex of degree 1: if ell(G) + br(G) < |V(G)| then br(G) = 0. Equivalently, every connected pendant-free graph with at least one bridge satisfies ell(G) + br(G) >= |V(G)|. (d = shortest-path metric; [uvw] :<=> d(u,v)+d(v,w)=d(u,w); line(a,b) := {a,b} u {x : [abx] or [axb] or [xab]}; ell = number of distinct lines; br = number of bridges.)

rows.title: Diameter confinement: every pendant-free bad graph has diameter <= 3
rows.statement: For every finite connected simple graph G with no vertex of degree 1 and diam(G) >= 4: ell(G) + br(G) >= |V(G)|. (d = shortest-path metric; [uvw] :<=> d(u,v)+d(v,w)=d(u,w); line(a,b) := {a,b} u {x : [abx] or [axb] or [xab]}; ell = number of distinct lines; br = number of bridges; diam(G) = max_{u,v} d(u,v).)

rows.title: H5 lemma: pendant-free + diam >= 4 implies ell >= n
rows.statement: For every finite connected simple graph G with no vertex of degree 1 and diam(G) >= 4: ell(G) >= |V(G)|. (d = shortest-path metric; [uvw] :<=> d(u,v)+d(v,w)=d(u,w); line(a,b) := {a,b} u {x in V : [abx] or [axb] or [xab]} for a != b; ell(G) = number of distinct lines.)
rows.notes: Plan coverage target 'H5 (Chen-Chvatal)' — present.

rows.title: Pencil charge (refuted): some vertex pencil reaches n
rows.statement: For every finite connected simple graph G with no vertex of degree 1 and diam(G) >= 4, there exists a vertex v in V(G) with F_pencil(v) := #{ line(v,w) : w in V(G) \ {v} } >= |V(G)| (lines as in row H5; the original claim used peripheral v, i.e. ecc(v) = diam(G), and the refutation also covers the stronger max over ALL vertices).

rows.title: Pair-indexed charge (refuted): diametral-pair lines reach n
rows.statement: For every finite connected simple graph G with no vertex of degree 1 and diam(G) >= 4: f_far0(G) := #{ line(a,b) : a,b in V(G), d(a,b) = diam(G) } >= |V(G)| (lines as in row H5; the refutation equally covers the companion pair functionals f_far1/f_far2 = distinct lines from pairs at distance >= diam-1 / diam-2, f_periphery = distinct lines among pairs of eccentricity-diam vertices, and f_match = distinct lines of a greedy far-pair matching).

rows.title: Pendant-free + diam >= 4 implies twin-free
rows.statement: For every finite connected simple graph G with no vertex of degree 1 and diam(G) >= 4, G has no metric twins: there are no distinct vertices u != v with d(u,x) = d(v,x) for every x in V(G) \ {u,v}, where d is the shortest-path metric.

rows.title: Twin-free + min-degree 2 does NOT imply ell >= n (refuted control claim)
rows.statement: For every finite connected simple graph G with minimum degree >= 2 and no metric twins (no distinct u,v with d(u,x) = d(v,x) for all x in V(G) \ {u,v}): ell(G) >= |V(G)| (lines/ell as in row H5).

rows.title: Lexicographic product G_2[G_2] stays in C_3 (disproved: explicit TT3)
rows.statement: The lexicographic substitution product G_2[G_2] belongs to the class C_3, where G_2 is the directed triangle on {0,1,2} with arcs 0->1, 1->2, 2->0, and G_2[G_2] is the 9-vertex digraph on vertex set {0,1,2} x {0,1,2} with intra-block arcs (i,u)->(i,v) for every i and every arc u->v of G_2, plus ALL nine inter-block arcs (i,u)->(j,v) for every arc i->j of G_2; C_3 = oriented digraphs (no digon) with no transitive triangle TT3 (no distinct u,v,w with arcs u->v, v->w, u->w) and no induced directed cycle of length >= 4. (Membership would make G_2[G_2] a 9-vertex C_3 digraph with dichromatic number 3, i.e. m(3) <= 9.)

rows.title: Directed-degeneracy threshold N(3) >= 10 (disproved: cyclic K_{3,3,3})
rows.statement: Every digraph D on at most 9 vertices in the class C_3 (oriented, no digon; no transitive triangle TT3, i.e. no distinct u,v,w with arcs u->v, v->w, u->w; no induced directed cycle of length >= 4) has directed degeneracy dd(D) <= 2, where dd(D) := max over induced subdigraphs H of D of min over v in V(H) of min(d+_H(v), d-_H(v)) (d+/d- = out-/in-degree in H). Equivalently N(3) >= 10, where N(t) := min{ |V(D)| : D in C_3 and dd(D) >= t }. (This was the falsifiable prediction of the route conjecturing N(t) super-linear, aiming at an asymptotic lower bound m(k) >= N(k-1) via the sound inequality chi_vec <= 1 + dd.)

rows.title: Lemma L: chi_vec <= omega(underlying) - 1 on C_3 (disproved: edgeless digraph)
rows.statement: For every digraph D in the class C_3 (oriented, no digon; no transitive triangle TT3, i.e. no distinct u,v,w with arcs u->v, v->w, u->w; no induced directed cycle of length >= 4), the dichromatic number satisfies chi_vec(D) <= omega(U(D)) - 1, where U(D) is the underlying simple graph of D (edge {u,v} present iff u->v or v->u is an arc of D), omega denotes the clique number, and chi_vec(D) is the least k such that V(D) partitions into k parts each inducing an acyclic subdigraph.

rows.title: Small 3-dichromatic C_3 witness: m(3) <= 20
rows.statement: There exists a digraph D on at most 20 vertices such that (i) D is oriented (no loops and no digons: never both arcs u->v and v->u), (ii) D contains no transitive triangle TT3 (no three distinct vertices u,v,w with all three arcs u->v, v->w, u->w), (iii) D contains no induced directed cycle of length >= 4 (no vertex subset S with |S| >= 4 whose induced subdigraph is exactly a directed cycle on S) -- i.e. D belongs to the class C_3 of arXiv:2202.01006 -- and (iv) the dichromatic number chi_vec(D) >= 3, where chi_vec(D) is the least k such that V(D) can be partitioned into k parts each inducing an acyclic subdigraph. Equivalently, m(3) <= 20, where m(k) := min{ |V(D)| : D in C_3 and chi_vec(D) >= k }.
rows.notes: Plan coverage target 'm(3) bounds handle' (upper half) — present; concrete derived handle for the vague S2 open problem (plan B4/B6).

rows.title: Every C_3 digraph on at most 10 vertices is 2-dicolourable (m(3) >= 11)
rows.statement: For every digraph D on at most 10 vertices belonging to the class C_3 -- D oriented (no loops, no digons: never both arcs u->v and v->u), containing no transitive triangle TT3 (no distinct u,v,w with arcs u->v, v->w, u->w), and containing no induced directed cycle of length >= 4 (no S subset of V(D), |S| >= 4, whose induced subdigraph is exactly a directed cycle) -- the dichromatic number satisfies chi_vec(D) <= 2, where chi_vec(D) is the least k such that V(D) partitions into k parts each inducing an acyclic subdigraph. Equivalently m(3) >= 11, where m(k) := min{ |V(D)| : D in C_3 and chi_vec(D) >= k }.
rows.notes: Plan coverage target 'm(3) bounds handle' (lower half) — present.

rows.title: Ore-corner certification at (26,51): every 26-Ore graph on 51 vertices has cr >= Z(26) = 5148
rows.statement: For a finite simple graph G, let cr(G) denote the crossing number: the minimum, over all drawings of G in the plane (vertices as distinct points, edges as arcs, no three edges through a common interior point), of the number of pairs of edges that cross. Let O_26 be the smallest class of graphs containing K_26 and closed under DHGO (Ore) composition with k = 26: given G1, G2 in O_k, an edge xy in E(G1) whose endpoint y has deg_{G1}(y) = k-1, a vertex z in V(G2) with deg_{G2}(z) = k-1, and a partition N_{G2}(z) = A ⊔ B with A, B nonempty, the composition G1 * G2 is obtained from the disjoint union of G1 - xy and (G2 - z) by adding the edges {xa : a in A} and {yb : b in B} (so |V(G1 * G2)| = |V(G1)| + |V(G2)| - 1; the vertices of A and B remain distinct). CLAIM: every G in O_26 with |V(G)| = 51 satisfies cr(G) >= 5148, where 5148 = Z(26) := (1/4)·floor(26/2)·floor(25/2)·floor(24/2)·floor(23/2) = 13·12·12·11/4 (Guy–Zarankiewicz quantity). Finite handle: by the D4 enumeration (in-folder, pynauty-canonical-form certified), O_26 restricted to 51 vertices is exactly the 12 pairwise non-isomorphic one-step compositions K_26 * K_26 parameterised by the partition size |A| = a in {1,...,12}, stored as graph6 lines in deliverables/D4_ore_26_51/ore_26_51.g6 (each with 51 vertices, 649 edges, minimum degree 25); the claim is a conjunction of 12 decidable statements cr(O_a) >= 5148.

rows.title: List-chromatic Albertson at t <= 18 (D15 main theorem / D14 T1'; refuted at t = 5)
rows.statement: For every finite simple graph G and every integer t with 1 <= t <= 18: if chi_l(G) >= t then cr(G) >= cr(K_t). Here chi_l(G) (list-chromatic / choice number) is the least k such that for every assignment of colour lists L(v) with |L(v)| = k for every vertex v, G admits a proper colouring c with c(v) in L(v) for all v; cr is the crossing number (minimum number of edge-pair crossings over plane drawings). DISPROVED at the instance t = 5: Voigt (1993) constructs a planar graph G_V that is not 4-choosable; planar graphs are 5-choosable (Thomassen 1994), so chi_l(G_V) = 5, while cr(G_V) = 0 < 1 = cr(K_5).

rows.title: FPS Claim 3.7 free-delta improvement (D5 headline: some delta beats 9/16; refuted by D8 witness identity)
rows.statement: Define f(alpha, beta, gamma; delta) := gamma - alpha·(delta - 1)/(delta - gamma), and for delta in (1, 5/4) let F(delta) := sup { f(alpha, beta, gamma; delta) : 0 <= alpha <= beta <= 1, 0 <= gamma <= alpha + (delta - alpha)(1 - beta)/(2 - beta) } — the Fox–Pach–Suk Claim 3.7 optimisation (arXiv:2510.05893, p. 9) with the degree-threshold parameter delta freed (FPS prove F(9/8) = 9/16; the admissible range (1, 5/4) comes from FPS Claim 3.6's hypothesis 4d < 5k). REFUTED CLAIM (D5 headline, from the D3 §7 re-tuning hypothesis): there exists delta in (1, 5/4) with F(delta) < 9/16 — specifically F(delta_1) ≈ 0.557454 at delta_1 ≈ 1.114907, the root in (1, 5/4) of delta^3 + 3·delta^2 - delta - 4 = 0. DISPROOF (D8 Theorem 1): F(delta) >= 9/16 for every delta in (1, 5/4), with equality iff delta = 9/8; the load-bearing certificate is the witness identity f_2b(4/7, delta) - 9/16 = 12·(delta - 9/8)^2 / [7·(4·delta - 1)] = 3·(8·delta - 9)^2 / [112·(4·delta - 1)] >= 0, where f_2b(eta, delta) is f evaluated at the explicit feasible point alpha = beta = 2 - 1/eta, gamma = alpha + (delta - alpha)(1 - beta)/(2 - beta) (the Case-2b boundary; at eta = 4/7: alpha = beta = 1/4). The refuted claim rested on the silent assumption that the Case-2b maximum equals delta/2 at every delta, which fails on (1, -3 + sqrt(17)) ∋ delta_1 (monotonicity transition at delta_crit = -3 + sqrt(17) ≈ 1.12311).

rows.title: 25-Ore family at (25,48) is nonempty (Role 2 claim '12 Ore classes / essentially a single graph'; refuted by Ore-order congruence)
rows.statement: There exists a graph G in O_25 with |V(G)| = 48, where O_25 is the smallest class of graphs containing K_25 and closed under DHGO (Ore) composition with k = 25 (composition as defined in Kostochka–Yancey arXiv:1209.1050 §2: pick xy in E(G1) with deg_{G1}(y) = 24, z in V(G2) with deg_{G2}(z) = 24, partition N_{G2}(z) = A ⊔ B nonempty, delete xy and z, add edges {xa : a in A} and {yb : b in B}; |V(G1 * G2)| = |V(G1)| + |V(G2)| - 1). DISPROVED: every G in O_k satisfies |V(G)| ≡ 1 (mod k - 1), by induction on the composition tree (base case |V(K_k)| = k ≡ 1 mod k-1; the composition step preserves the congruence since n1 + n2 - 1 ≡ 1 + 1 - 1 = 1 mod k-1); for k = 25 this forces |V| in {25, 49, 73, ...}, and 48 ≡ 0 (mod 24) ≠ 1. In particular K_25 * K_25 has order 49, not 48; the 25-Ore family on 48 vertices is empty.

rows.title: Delta = 4 case of Cheng-Keevash Conjecture 1 (full closure at all n)
rows.statement: Let D = (V, A) be a finite digraph (A a set of ordered pairs of distinct vertices; write u -> v for (u,v) in A). D is an oriented graph if for no pair of distinct vertices u, v both u -> v and v -> u are arcs (no 2-cycles). For v in V, d+(v) is the number of arcs with tail v, and delta+(D) := min_{v in V} d+(v). A directed path of length k is a sequence of k+1 pairwise-distinct vertices v_0, v_1, ..., v_k with v_{i-1} -> v_i for every 1 <= i <= k; the length is the number of arcs k, not the number of vertices. Proposition: every finite oriented graph D with delta+(D) >= 4 contains a directed path of length 8.
rows.notes: Parent note (applies to all 5 dirpath rows): the folder is the directed_path/Cheng-Keevash problem; the v2 manifest has NO rows for arXiv 2402.16776, but the v1 OPG row 'directed_cycle_of_length_twice_the_minimum_outdegree' states Conjecture 1 verbatim ('Every oriented graph with minimum outdegree k contains a directed path of length 2k') and is used as parent.

rows.title: Delta = 4, n = 12 frontier case (first genuinely open size)
rows.statement: Let D = (V, A) be a finite oriented graph (a digraph with no 2-cycle: for no distinct u, v are both u -> v and v -> u arcs). d+(v) is the number of arcs with tail v and delta+(D) := min_v d+(v). A directed path of length k is a sequence of k+1 pairwise-distinct vertices v_0, ..., v_k with v_{i-1} -> v_i for 1 <= i <= k (length = number of arcs). Proposition: every oriented graph D with delta+(D) >= 4 and |V(D)| = 12 contains a directed path of length 8.

rows.title: Jackson 1981 minimum-semidegree theorem (transcription unverified; standing working hypothesis)
rows.statement: Let D be a finite oriented graph (no 2-cycles) and k >= 1 an integer. Let d+(v), d-(v) be the numbers of arcs with tail v and head v respectively, delta+(D) := min_v d+(v), delta-(D) := min_v d-(v), and delta0(D) := min(delta+(D), delta-(D)) the minimum semidegree. A directed Hamilton cycle is a directed cycle passing through every vertex of D exactly once; a directed path of length 2k is a sequence of 2k+1 pairwise-distinct vertices each sending an arc to the next. Proposition (Jackson 1981): if delta0(D) >= k, then (i) D contains a directed Hamilton cycle when |V(D)| <= 2k+2, and (ii) D contains a directed path of length 2k when |V(D)| > 2k+2.

rows.title: '+1 strengthening' of Cheng-Keevash Lemma 7
rows.statement: Let delta >= 1 be an integer and D a finite oriented graph (no 2-cycles) with delta+(D) >= delta, where delta+(D) := min_v d+(v) and d+(v) is the number of arcs with tail v. Let l(D) denote the maximum number of arcs in a directed simple path of D (a sequence of pairwise-distinct vertices each sending an arc to the next). For an induced subgraph S of D (the sub-digraph on a vertex subset with all arcs of D between its vertices), delta+(S) := min_{v in V(S)} |{w in V(S) : v -> w}|. Proposition: either D contains a directed path of length 2*delta, or D has an induced subgraph S with |V(S)| <= delta and delta+(S) >= 2*delta + 1 - l(D).

rows.title: Thomasse girth strengthening (refuted for g >= 4 by explicit D_{a,b} counterexamples)
rows.statement: Let D be a finite digraph with delta+(D) >= delta (delta+(D) := min_v d+(v), d+(v) the number of arcs with tail v) and girth g, where the girth is the minimum number of arcs in a directed cycle of D. Thomasse's girth strengthening asserts: D contains a directed simple path of length delta*(g-1) (a sequence of delta*(g-1)+1 pairwise-distinct vertices each sending an arc to the next). DISPROVED for every g >= 4 (with delta >= 2): define the k-lift of a vertex v as deleting all arcs with tail v, adding k-1 pairwise-disjoint sets U_{v,1}, ..., U_{v,k-1} of delta new vertices each, setting U_{v,0} := {v} and U_{v,k} := N+(v), and adding all arcs from U_{v,i-1} to U_{v,i} for 1 <= i <= k (a 1-lift does nothing; every lift preserves all out-degrees = delta). Let D_{a,b} be the complete digraph on delta+1 vertices with one vertex a-lifted and every other vertex b-lifted. Then (Claim 6) D_{a,b} has girth a+b and longest directed path of length exactly delta*b + a - 1. Taking a = b = g/2 for even g, or a = (g-1)/2 and b = (g+1)/2 for odd g, yields for every g >= 4 and delta >= 2 a digraph of girth g with delta+ = delta whose longest path is shorter than delta*(g-1). Smallest instance: g = 4, delta = 2 gives D_{2,2} on 9 vertices with longest path 5 < 6 = delta*(g-1).

rows.title: Theorem A (single-tool): no biplanar 10-chromatic odd-cycle clique blowup with n ≤ 30, ω ≤ 8
rows.statement: For all integers r ≥ 1 and a_1, …, a_{2r+1} ≥ 1, let G := C_{2r+1}[K_{a_1}, …, K_{a_{2r+1}}] be the weighted odd-cycle clique blowup: V(G) = F_1 ⊔ … ⊔ F_{2r+1} with |F_i| = a_i ('fibres'), and distinct x ∈ F_i, y ∈ F_j adjacent iff i = j or i − j ≡ ±1 (mod 2r+1). If n := Σ_i a_i ≤ 30, the clique number ω(G) ≤ 8 (for r ≥ 2 this equals max_i (a_i + a_{i+1 mod 2r+1}); it implies G is K_9-free), χ(G) ≥ 10 (no proper 9-coloring), and |E(G)| ≤ 6n − 12, then G is not biplanar, i.e., E(G) admits no partition into two sets each forming a planar graph on V(G).

rows.title: Sub-bracket chi_EM >= 10 (refute folklore chi_EM = 9)
rows.statement: There exists a finite simple graph G = (V, E) that is biplanar — i.e., E admits a partition E = E1 ⊔ E2 such that both (V, E1) and (V, E2) are planar (embeddable in the plane with no edge crossings; equivalently containing neither K_5 nor K_{3,3} as a minor) — and that admits no proper vertex coloring with 9 colors, i.e., there is no map c : V → {1,…,9} with c(u) ≠ c(v) for every uv ∈ E. Equivalently, chi_EM := max{ χ(G) : G biplanar } ≥ 10, where χ(G) is the least k admitting a proper k-coloring.
rows.notes: Plan coverage target 'chi_EM sub-brackets' (lower) — present.

rows.title: Sub-bracket chi_EM <= 11
rows.statement: For every finite simple graph G = (V, E) that is biplanar — i.e., E admits a partition E = E1 ⊔ E2 with both (V, E1) and (V, E2) planar (no-crossing plane embedding; equivalently no K_5 or K_{3,3} minor) — there exists a proper vertex coloring c : V → {1,…,11} (c(u) ≠ c(v) whenever uv ∈ E). Equivalently chi_EM := max{ χ(G) : G biplanar } ≤ 11.
rows.notes: Plan coverage target 'chi_EM sub-brackets' (upper) — present.

rows.title: Target density lemma: 12-critical K_9-free graphs have at least 6n − 11 edges
rows.statement: For every finite simple graph G on n = |V(G)| vertices such that (i) G is 12-critical — χ(G) = 12 (G has a proper 12-coloring but no proper 11-coloring) and every proper subgraph H ⊊ G satisfies χ(H) ≤ 11 — and (ii) G contains no K_9, i.e., no set of 9 pairwise-adjacent vertices, the edge count satisfies |E(G)| ≥ 6n − 11 (equivalently |E(G)| > 6n − 12).

rows.title: Fork C capacity claim: every 35-edge high-only K_7-chunk profile is non-biplanar (REFUTED)
rows.statement: REFUTED CLAIM: every finite simple graph G with V(G) = C ⊔ A such that G[C] ≅ K_7, A is an independent set in G, every edge incident to a vertex of A has its other endpoint in C, and the number of C–A edges equals 35, fails to be biplanar (its edge set admits no partition into two planar subgraphs on V(G)). COUNTEREXAMPLE (explicit in docs/phase6_discharge_attempt.md §7.16): C = {c_0,…,c_6} inducing K_7, A = {a_0,…,a_6}, with a_i adjacent to exactly the five vertices c_j, j ∉ {i, i+1 (mod 7)} (each outside vertex misses one consecutive cyclic pair); n = 14, m = C(7,2) + 7·5 = 56; this balanced cyclic profile was reported biplanar (SMS SAT, 0.011 s).

rows.title: Q0 closure: no self-reproducing contraction scenario at n = 89
rows.statement: There is no pair (G, v) such that: (i) G is a finite simple graph that is 12-critical (χ(G) = 12, every proper subgraph 11-colorable), contains no K_9 (no 9 pairwise-adjacent vertices), and is biplanar (E(G) partitions into two planar subgraphs on V(G)), with |V(G)| = 89 and |E(G)| = 522 = 6·89 − 12; (ii) v ∈ V(G) has deg_G(v) = 11 and every neighbor of v has degree ≥ 12; (iii) the induced subgraph G[N(v)] is a vertex-disjoint union of complete graphs (necessarily each of order ≤ 7, since adding v would otherwise create a K_9); and (iv) for every pair of non-adjacent vertices u, u' ∈ N(v) lying in distinct components (cliques) of G[N(v)], the simple graph G* obtained from G − v by identifying u with u' (merging parallel edges) is itself a 12-critical K_9-free graph on 87 vertices with exactly 511 edges (the edge count 511 = 522 − 11 − q forces q = 0, i.e., u and u' have no common neighbor in G − v).

rows.title: Step 4+: some q ≥ 1 non-edge in N(v) contracts K_9-safely
rows.statement: Let G be any finite simple graph that is 12-critical (χ(G) = 12 and every proper subgraph is 11-colorable), K_9-free (no 9 pairwise-adjacent vertices), and biplanar (E(G) partitions into two planar subgraphs on V(G)). Let L := {x ∈ V(G) : deg_G(x) = 11} and suppose v ∈ L is isolated in the induced subgraph G[L], i.e., no neighbor of v has degree 11 (so |N(v)| = 11 and N(v) contains at least 6 non-adjacent pairs). For distinct non-adjacent u, u' ∈ N(v), set q(u,u') := |N_{G−v}(u) ∩ N_{G−v}(u')| (number of common neighbors in G − v), and call the non-edge {u,u'} K_9-safe if the simple graph obtained from G − v by identifying u with u' (replacing parallel edges by single edges) contains no K_9. CLAIM: if at least one non-edge {u,u'} ⊆ N(v) has q(u,u') ≥ 1, then at least one non-edge {u,u'} ⊆ N(v) with q(u,u') ≥ 1 is K_9-safe (equivalently, admits no 'split obstruction': no K_8 in G − v inside N(u) ∪ N(u') meeting both N(u)∖N(u') and N(u')∖N(u)).

rows.title: G1 — refuted: every in-class digraph has maximum out-degree <= 1 (functional-digraph lemma)
rows.statement: REFUTED CLAIM: every finite digraph in Forb_ind({digon, ->C3, S2+}) (definitions as in row H1) has maximum out-degree at most 1, i.e. is a functional digraph. EXPLICIT COUNTEREXAMPLE: the transitive tournament TT3 on vertices {0,1,2} with arcs 0->1, 0->2, 1->2 is in the class (oriented; no directed triangle; every out-neighbourhood induces a tournament, so no induced S2+) and vertex 0 has out-degree 2. More generally TT_k is in-class with out-degree k-1 (TT4 gives out-degree 3). Root cause: the class forbids only the DIRECTED triangle, not the transitive triangle, so out-neighbourhoods can be transitive tournaments of any size.

rows.title: G11 — refuted: min-FAS nesting lemma (some minimum feedback-arc-set order has a cut crossed by <= 1 back-arc)
rows.statement: REFUTED CLAIM: for every finite digraph D in Forb_ind({digon, ->C3, S2+}) (definitions as in row H1) on n vertices, there exist a linear order pi of V(D) minimizing the number of back-arcs (arcs u->v with pi(v) < pi(u); i.e. a minimum feedback-arc-set order) and a cut position c in {1,...,n-1} such that at most one back-arc crosses the cut (i.e. at most one back-arc u->v has pi(v) <= c < pi(u)) — 'crossing-width <= 1 at some cut'. EXPLICIT COUNTEREXAMPLE: C7(2,4), the circulant on Z_7 with arcs i->i+2 and i->i+4 (mod 7), arc list [[0,2],[3,0],[0,4],[5,0],[1,3],[4,1],[1,5],[6,1],[2,4],[5,2],[2,6],[3,5],[6,3],[4,6]] (as recorded in the ledger): it is in-class, vertex-transitive, chi_d = 2, has minimum back-arc count 3, and over ALL minimum-FAS orders every cut is crossed by at least 2 back-arcs; the best achievable min-over-cuts crossing-width is 2 (e.g. the order (0,2,4,6,1,3,5) has per-cut widths [2,3,3,3,3,2]).

rows.title: G12 — refuted: parity/alternate-interval 2-colouring of round in-class members
rows.statement: REFUTED CLAIM: for every ROUND member D of Forb_ind({digon, ->C3, S2+}) (round as defined in row H7, with round cyclic labelling v_0,...,v_{n-1}), the parity-along-round-order 2-colouring — v_i coloured i mod 2, i.e. the partition into even-indexed and odd-indexed vertices — is an acyclic 2-partition (each colour class induces a subdigraph with no directed cycle). EXPLICIT COUNTEREXAMPLE: C10(1,2,3) (arcs i->i+1, i->i+2, i->i+3 mod 10) is in-class and round in its natural cyclic labelling, but the even class {0,2,4,6,8} induces the directed 5-cycle 0->2->4->6->8->0 via the jump-2 arcs (and likewise the odd class), so the parity partition is not acyclic. The ledger records the parity rule invalid for C_n(1,2,3) for every n = 10..15, whereas a single contiguous interval of size 3 does give a valid 2-dicolouring of C10(1,2,3) (50 valid interval-partitions).

rows.title: G8 — refuted: every nontrivial strong component of an in-class member has a vertex of in-degree <= 2
rows.statement: REFUTED CLAIM: for every finite digraph D in Forb_ind({digon, ->C3, S2+}) (definitions as in row H1), every strongly connected induced subdigraph with at least 2 vertices (every nontrivial strong component) contains a vertex whose in-degree within that subdigraph is at most 2 (and symmetrically a vertex of out-degree at most 2), so that strong components are 2-in-degenerate and peelable. EXPLICIT COUNTEREXAMPLE: C10(1,2,3), the circulant digraph on Z_10 with arcs i->i+1, i->i+2, i->i+3 (mod 10), is in the class (oriented, no directed triangle, every N+(x) = {x+1,x+2,x+3} induces a transitive tournament), is a single strong component on all 10 vertices, and is 3-in-regular AND 3-out-regular (minimum in-degree = minimum out-degree = 3 everywhere), with girth 4 and chi_d = 2. No vertex of in-degree <= 2 exists, so no degeneracy/peeling order exists.

rows.title: Conjecture 6.2 — chi_d(Forb_ind(digon, ->C3, S2+)) = 2
rows.statement: Every finite loopless digraph D that (a) contains no digon (no pair of vertices u != v with both arcs u->v and v->u; equivalently D is an oriented graph), (b) contains no directed triangle ->C3 (no vertices a,b,c with a->b, b->c, c->a; in an oriented graph a directed triangle is automatically induced), and (c) contains no induced out-star S2+ (no three distinct vertices x,a,b with x->a and x->b such that there is no arc between a and b in either direction and no arc a->x or b->x; in an oriented graph this is equivalent to: some vertex x has two out-neighbours joined by no arc, i.e. some out-neighbourhood N+(x) does not induce a tournament) satisfies chi_d(D) <= 2, where the dichromatic number chi_d(D) is the least k such that V(D) can be partitioned into k sets each inducing a subdigraph with no directed cycle. Since the directed 4-cycle belongs to the class and has chi_d = 2, this is equivalent to chi_d(Forb_ind({digon, ->C3, S2+})) = 2 exactly.
rows.notes: Plan coverage target 'Conjecture 6.2' — present. Verified non-duplicate: v2 manifest has no Conjecture 6.2 row for 2009.13319 (only Problem 1.2 / Conj 4.2 / Conj 4.4 / research direction). Rocq anchor conj_6_2 already exists in heroes_dichotomy.v.

rows.title: Class identity — Forb_ind(digon, ->C3, S2+) = oriented ->C3-free out-local-tournaments with transitive out-neighbourhoods
rows.statement: For every finite loopless digraph D the following are equivalent: (1) D contains no induced digon, no induced directed triangle ->C3, and no induced out-star S2+ (definitions as in row H1); (2) D is an oriented graph with no directed 3-cycle such that for every vertex x the out-neighbourhood N+(x) = {y : x->y} induces a tournament (every two out-neighbours are joined by exactly one arc), i.e. D is an out-local-tournament. Moreover, in any D satisfying (2), every N+(x) induces a TRANSITIVE tournament (a tournament whose arc relation is transitive; equivalently, a tournament with no directed triangle), since a directed triangle inside N+(x) would be a directed triangle of D.

rows.title: HALF A — contiguous interval of length d and its complement 2-dicolour the circulant C_n<1..d>
rows.statement: For all integers d >= 1 and n >= 2d+1, let C_n<1..d> denote the circulant digraph with vertex set Z_n and arc set {i -> (i+j) mod n : i in Z_n, j in {1,...,d}}. Then for every a in Z_n the contiguous interval I_a = {a, a+1, ..., a+d-1} (residues mod n) and its complement Z_n \ I_a each induce an acyclic subdigraph of C_n<1..d> (no directed cycle); hence the partition {I_a, Z_n \ I_a} witnesses chi_d(C_n<1..d>) <= 2 uniformly in n. (By rotation-invariance of circulants the choice of a is immaterial.) This is 'HALF A' of ledger entry H6: the closed-form acyclic 2-partition of the dense round in-class circulants — the residual hard family of Conjecture 6.2, concrete model C10(1,2,3) = C_10<1..3> — covering in particular every C_n<1..d> that lies in Forb_ind({digon, ->C3, S2+}) (the ->C3-free ones, d roughly <= n/3).
rows.notes: Plan coverage target 'HALF A' — present. HALF B has no candidate row (see coverage_gaps).

rows.title: Localization identity — non-round = non-locally-in-semicomplete = embedded in-star, for strong in-class members
rows.statement: For every strongly connected finite digraph D in Forb_ind({digon, ->C3, S2+}) (definitions as in row H1) the following are equivalent: (1) D is NOT round, where a digraph D on vertices labelled v_0,...,v_{n-1} is ROUND if there is a cyclic labelling such that for every i, N+(v_i) = {v_{i+1}, ..., v_{i+d+(v_i)}} and N-(v_i) = {v_{i-d-(v_i)}, ..., v_{i-1}} with indices mod n and d+(v_i), d-(v_i) the out- and in-degrees (each out-neighbourhood is a segment of consecutive vertices immediately following v_i, each in-neighbourhood a segment immediately preceding v_i); (2) D is NOT locally in-semicomplete: some in-neighbourhood N-(v) = {u : u->v} does not induce a tournament; (3) D has a vertex v whose in-neighbourhood contains an independent pair — two vertices a, b with a->v and b->v and no arc between a and b in either direction (an embedded in-star S2-, where S2- is the 3-vertex digraph {1,2}->0). ((2)<=>(3) is definitional in an oriented graph; the substantive content is (1)<=>(2): the round strong in-class members are exactly the locally-in-semicomplete ones.)

rows.title: Cyclic substitution preserves induced-P+(2)-freeness for all cycle lengths (refuted: directed C4)
rows.statement: For every integer k >= 3 and all finite digraphs D_1, ..., D_k each containing no induced copy of P+(2) (the directed path on 3 vertices {0,1,2} with arcs (0,1),(1,2) and 0, 2 non-adjacent), the cyclic substitution C_k(D_1,...,D_k) — disjoint copies of D_1, ..., D_k plus, for each i, all arcs from every vertex of copy i to every vertex of copy (i+1 mod k) — contains no induced copy of P+(2). (This 'general bundle mechanism' was proposed to explain why the tournament tower avoids induced P+(2) in any C_k-substitution.)

rows.title: C4-substitution tower stays triangle-free / in Forb_ind(K2,K3,P+(3)) (refuted: T_3 contains TT3)
rows.statement: Every member of the C4-substitution tower defined by T_1 = K1 (one vertex) and T_{i+1} = C_4(T_i, T_i, T_i, T_i) — substitution into the directed 4-cycle: four disjoint copies of T_i plus all arcs from every vertex of copy j to every vertex of copy (j+1 mod 4), for j = 0,1,2,3 — is triangle-free, i.e., contains no induced orientation of K3 (no three pairwise adjacent vertices; forbids both TT3, the transitive 3-vertex tournament, and the directed 3-cycle C3); hence every T_i, being also digon-free and free of induced P+(3) (directed path on 4 vertices, arcs (0,1),(1,2),(2,3), no other adjacencies) with chi_d(T_i) = i, lies in Forb_ind(K2, K3, P+(3)) and witnesses that {K2, K3, P+(3)} is not heroic (a disproof of H1 at k = 3).

rows.title: Long chordless directed cycles avoid induced P+(4) (refuted: C6 contains P+(4))
rows.statement: For every integer m >= 6, the directed cycle on m vertices — vertex set {0,...,m-1}, arc set {(i, (i+1) mod m)}, no other arcs — contains no induced copy of P+(4), the directed path on 5 vertices {0,...,4} with arc set {(0,1),(1,2),(2,3),(3,4)} and no other adjacencies (induced containment: an injection f such that for all ordered pairs of distinct x, y, (x,y) is an arc of P+(4) iff (f(x),f(y)) is an arc of the host). (This was the load-bearing step of the G6 reduction: it would imply the Harutyunyan-Mohar high-digirth triangle-free digon-free family with unbounded chi_d avoids induced P+(4), hence lies in Forb_ind(K2, K3, P+(4)), disproving H1 at k = 4 and making P+(3) vs P+(4) the exact heroic boundary.)

rows.title: {K2, K3, P+(k)} is heroic for every k (sufficiency instance of Conjecture 4.2)
rows.statement: For every integer k >= 1 there exists a constant c(k) such that every finite digraph D satisfying (i) D has no digon (no distinct vertices u, v with both arcs (u,v) and (v,u); i.e., D is an oriented graph, equivalently no induced K2sym), (ii) D is triangle-free: no three distinct vertices are pairwise adjacent, where u, v are adjacent iff at least one of (u,v), (v,u) is an arc — equivalently D contains no induced copy of any orientation of K3, i.e., neither TT3 (the transitive tournament on 3 vertices, arcs (0,1),(0,2),(1,2)) nor the directed 3-cycle C3, and (iii) D contains no induced copy of P+(k), the directed path on k+1 vertices {0,...,k} with arc set {(i,i+1) : 0 <= i < k} and no other adjacencies (induced containment: an injection f with (x,y) an arc of P+(k) iff (f(x),f(y)) an arc of D, for all ordered pairs of distinct vertices), has dichromatic number chi_d(D) <= c(k), where chi_d(D) is the least r such that V(D) partitions into r classes each inducing a subdigraph with no directed cycle. Equivalently: the set {K2, K3, P+(k)} is heroic for every fixed k.
rows.notes: Plan coverage target 'H1 (heroic {K2,K3,P+(k)})' — present. Proper sub-instance of Conj 4.2/4.4, not equal to any S2 row. Heroic-siblings dedupe: no proposition shared with heroic_sets_hero_forest.

rows.title: {K2, P+(m)} is non-heroic for every m >= 2 (tournament tower)
rows.statement: For every integer m >= 2, the class Forb_ind(K2, P+(m)) — all finite digraphs with no digon (no distinct u, v carrying both arcs (u,v) and (v,u)) and no induced copy of P+(m), the directed path on m+1 vertices {0,...,m} with arcs {(i,i+1) : 0 <= i < m} and no other adjacencies — has unbounded dichromatic number chi_d (least number of classes in a vertex partition each inducing a directed-cycle-free subdigraph); i.e., {K2, P+(m)} is not heroic. Witness family: the tournament tower T_1 = K1 (one vertex, no arcs), T_j = C3(T_{j-1}, T_{j-1}, K1), where C3(A,B,C) is substitution into the directed 3-cycle: disjoint copies of A, B, C plus all arcs from every vertex of A to every vertex of B, from B to C, and from C to A. Each T_j is a tournament (every pair of vertices adjacent, no digon), hence contains no induced P+(m) for any m >= 2 (P+(m) has a non-adjacent pair), and chi_d(T_j) = j for every j >= 1.

rows.title: Triangle-free + digon-free digraphs with chi_d >= 3 exist ({K2, K3} not heroic with bound 2)
rows.statement: There exists a finite digraph D that is digon-free (no distinct vertices u, v with both arcs (u,v) and (v,u)) and triangle-free (no three distinct vertices pairwise adjacent, where adjacency means at least one arc between the pair; equivalently no induced orientation of K3, i.e., no TT3 and no directed 3-cycle) with dichromatic number chi_d(D) >= 3, where chi_d(D) is the least r such that V(D) partitions into r classes each inducing a subdigraph with no directed cycle. Equivalently: the set {K2, K3} is not heroic with bound 2. Any witness has at least 9 vertices: all orientations of all triangle-free graphs on n <= 8 vertices have chi_d <= 2 (exhaustive), and all 2^19 orientations of the Groetzsch graph (n = 11) have chi_d <= 2 (two independent exhaustive methods).

rows.title: Bridgeless counter-examples to ell(G)+br(G) >= |G| are finite
rows.statement: There exists N in NAT such that for every finite connected simple bridgeless graph G with n = |V(G)| >= N, ell(G) >= n. Equivalently (since there are only finitely many graphs of each order up to isomorphism): up to isomorphism there are only finitely many connected bridgeless graphs G with ell(G) + br(G) < |V(G)| (for bridgeless G this reads ell(G) < |V(G)|). Definitions: G is bridgeless iff it has no bridge, i.e. no edge e with G - e disconnected (so br(G) = 0); d(u,v) is the shortest-path (hop) distance in G; [p q r] holds iff d(p,q) + d(q,r) = d(p,r); for distinct vertices a,b, line(a,b) = {a,b} UNION {x in V(G) \ {a,b} : [a b x] or [a x b] or [x a b]}; ell(G) is the number of distinct sets line(a,b) over all unordered pairs {a,b} of distinct vertices.
rows.notes: MERGE: kept from the stale twin folder — distinct from active H2 (bounded deficit) and H3 (bad => bridgeless), and a strict restriction (bridgeless subclass) of parent Conjecture 2.2 (pendant-free finiteness), so neither an intra-attack nor an s2 duplicate.

rows.title: Lines+bridges inequality ell(G)+br(G) >= |G| (refuted; C4 certificate)
rows.statement: For every finite connected simple graph G with n = |V(G)| >= 2 vertices: ell(G) + br(G) >= n. Definitions: d(u,v) is the shortest-path (hop) distance in G; for vertices p,q,r the metric-betweenness relation [p q r] holds iff d(p,q) + d(q,r) = d(p,r); for distinct vertices a,b the metric line is line(a,b) = {a,b} UNION {x in V(G) \ {a,b} : [a b x] or [a x b] or [x a b]}; ell(G) is the number of DISTINCT sets line(a,b) (as vertex subsets) over all unordered pairs {a,b} of distinct vertices; a bridge of G is an edge e such that G - e is disconnected, and br(G) is the number of bridges of G.
rows.notes: MERGE: kept from the stale twin folder — no counterpart row exists in the active chen_chvatal_lines_plus_bridges candidates (the base-inequality refutation with the C4 T2 anchor is unique to this folder). Verified not an s2-duplicate: axv_1606_06011_00/_01 concern the structure of the counterexample set, not the inequality itself.

rows.title: Additive substitution upper bound omega_vec(S[H]) <= omega_vec(S)+omega_vec(H)-1
rows.statement: For all tournaments S and H, omega_vec(S[H]) <= omega_vec(S) + omega_vec(H) - 1, where S[H] is the lexicographic substitution (V(S[H]) = V(S) x V(H); arc (u,x)->(v,y) iff u->v in S, or u=v and x->y in H) and omega_vec(T) is the tournament clique number: the minimum over all total orders prec of V(T) of the clique number of the backedge graph T^prec (edge {u,v}, u prec v, iff the arc of T is v->u).

rows.title: Width-2 confinement: omega_vec(C3[H]) <= omega_vec(H)+1 whenever omega_vec(H) >= 3
rows.statement: For every tournament H with omega_vec(H) >= 3, omega_vec(C3[H]) <= omega_vec(H) + 1, where C3[H] is the lexicographic substitution of H into the directed 3-cycle (three disjoint copies H_0,H_1,H_2 of H, all arcs from H_i to H_{i+1 mod 3} between copies, arcs of H inside copies) and omega_vec(T) is the tournament clique number: the minimum over all total orders prec of V(T) of the clique number of the backedge graph T^prec (edge {u,v}, u prec v, iff the arc of T is v->u).
rows.notes: Kept despite t2_certificate=false: plan B6 drops only no-payoff entries; this refutation is itself a formalization target and pre-anchors a future finite certificate (e.g. SAT omega_vec(B_3) >= 5).

rows.title: Dic-gap barrier: the dichromatic-clique gap reopens under every C3 lift
rows.statement: For every tournament H with dic(H) = omega_vec(H), it holds that dic(C3[H]) > omega_vec(C3[H]). Here omega_vec(T) is the tournament clique number: the minimum over all total orders prec of V(T) of the clique number omega(T^prec) of the backedge graph T^prec, which has an edge {u,v} with u prec v iff the arc of T is v->u; dic(T) is the dichromatic number: the minimum number of parts in a partition of V(T) into sets each inducing an acyclic subdigraph; and C3[H] is the lexicographic substitution of H into the directed 3-cycle: three disjoint copies H_0,H_1,H_2 of H with every arc from H_i to H_{i+1 mod 3} between copies and the arcs of H inside copies. Consequence if true: the Aubian-Coulomb Prop 6.2 lift (if dic(H)=omega_vec(H)=k and dic(H-v)=k-1 for all v, then C3[H] is (k+1)-omega_vec-critical) can fire at most once per supply object and cannot be iterated.
rows.notes: Parent = Question 5.9 (axv_2310_04265_09): the folder README names Question 5.9 as its main target. Tournament-siblings dedupe vs tournament_twinwidth (same paper 2310.04265): no shared propositions found.

rows.title: Constant independence cap alpha <= 3 on optimal orders of 3-omega_vec-critical tournaments
rows.statement: Every 3-omega_vec-critical tournament T (omega_vec(T)=3 and omega_vec(T-v)=2 for every vertex v, where omega_vec(T) is the minimum over all total orders prec of V(T) of the clique number of the backedge graph T^prec, which has edge {u,v}, u prec v, iff the arc of T is v->u) admits an optimal order prec (one with omega(T^prec)=3) whose backedge graph T^prec has independence number at most 3.

rows.title: Pod-tightness of the iterated-triangle layer-product growth: lambda = 3/2
rows.statement: Let B_0 = TT_1 (a single vertex) and B_k = C3[B_{k-1}] (lexicographic substitution into the directed 3-cycle), so V(B_k) = {0,1,2}^k with arc x->y iff y_j = x_j + 1 (mod 3), where j is the first coordinate at which x and y differ; colour that arc c = x_j. For each c in {0,1,2} the colour-c arcs form a transitive partial order P_c. For a total order pi of V(B_k), let q_c(pi) be the maximum length r of a P_c-chain v_1 <_{P_c} ... <_{P_c} v_r that is pi-decreasing (v_r pi-before ... pi-before v_1), and set L_k = min over total orders pi of q_0(pi)*q_1(pi)*q_2(pi). The limit lambda = lim_{k->infty} L_k^{1/k} exists (L_k is submultiplicative) and satisfies 3/2 <= lambda <= 24^{1/5}. Conjecture (pod-tight side): lambda = 3/2, i.e. L_k = (3/2)^{k+o(k)}.

rows.title: Pack-coupled D25-ring reaches chi_vec >= 4 (n <= 175) — disproved, all 12 instances 3-dicolourable
rows.statement: There exist L in {3,5,7}, a pack offset d in {1,2}, and one of the two coded interface orientations such that the pack-coupled D25-ring — L disjoint copies of D25 = C5<-5 (defined as: vertices u_{p,j}, p in Z_5, j in {0..4}, arcs u_{p,j}->u_{p+1,j'} for j != j' and u_{p+1,j}->u_{p,j}) placed on a directed L-cycle, with, for each k in Z_L, a complete bipartite set of like-oriented arcs between one 5-vertex independent pack of copy k and the pack at position offset d of copy k+1 (exact parametrization: scripts/constructions.py:pack_coupled_d25_ring) — is an oriented triangle-free digraph with dichromatic number chi_vec >= 4 (giving m(4) <= 25L <= 175).

rows.title: Matched-shift substitution product D25<C_ell> reaches chi_vec >= 4 — disproved, chi_vec collapses to 2
rows.statement: There exists ell in {5,7} such that the matched-shift substitution product D25<C_ell> — replace each vertex u of D25 = C5<-5 (defined as in the D25 rows) by a directed ell-cycle on vertices (u,0),...,(u,ell-1) with arcs (u,i)->(u,i+1 mod ell), and for each arc u->v of D25 add the matched shift-by-1 interface arcs (u,i)->(v,i+1 mod ell) for all i — is an oriented triangle-free digraph on n = 25*ell vertices with dichromatic number chi_vec >= 4.

rows.title: Degree floor d+(v)*d-(v) >= 10 for 3-dichromatic digon-free digraphs — disproved by Paley P7
rows.statement: Every digon-free digraph D (no loops, no pair of opposite arcs) with dichromatic number chi_vec(D) >= 3 — chi_vec(D) is the least k such that V(D) partitions into k sets each inducing a subdigraph with no directed cycle — contains a vertex v with d+(v)*d-(v) >= 10 (out-degree times in-degree); equivalently max_v sqrt(d+(v)d-(v)) >= 3/(1-e^-13) = 3.0000068.

rows.title: Two-axis odd-cycle blowup of D25 is triangle-free with chi_vec >= 4 — disproved by an explicit triangle
rows.statement: For some ell in {3,5}, the two-axis blowup B_ell(D25) — replace each vertex x of D25 = C5<-5 (defined as in the D25 rows) by an independent pack of ell columns u_{x,0},...,u_{x,ell-1}; for each arc x->y of D25 add forward off-diagonal arcs u_{x,j}->u_{y,j'} for all j != j' and matched backward arcs u_{y,j}->u_{x,j}; and add in every pack the intra-pack directed cycle u_{x,j}->u_{x,j+1 mod ell} — is an oriented triangle-free digraph with dichromatic number chi_vec >= 4 (n = 75 or 125).

rows.title: Upper-bound transfer a_vec(D) <= alpha(G) for every orientation — disproved by directed C5
rows.statement: For every oriented graph D (digraph with no loops and no pair of opposite arcs) with underlying undirected graph G, the acyclic number alpha_vec(D) (maximum size of a set S of vertices such that D[S] contains no directed cycle) is at most the independence number alpha(G) of G.

rows.title: Grötzsch t-backward-blowup below t = 19 reaches chi_vec >= 4 (searched orientation grid) — disproved on every resolved instance
rows.statement: At least one of the following digraphs has dichromatic number chi_vec >= 4: the t-backward-blowup of an orientation Dor of the Grötzsch graph (the Mycielskian of C5: 11 vertices, 20 edges, triangle-free, chromatic number 4, independence number 5), where the t-backward-blowup replaces each vertex x by an independent pack of t columns x_0..x_{t-1} and each arc x->y by forward arcs x_j->y_{j'} for all j != j' plus matched backward arcs y_j->x_j (always oriented and triangle-free), ranging over the resolved search grid: all 32 searched orientations (the acyclic one, 30 random, balanced/Eulerian non-acyclic) for t in {2..10}; the acyclic, balanced-non-acyclic and random orientations for t in {11..15}; the acyclic and three random orientations for t = 16; the acyclic (lo-hi) orientation for t = 17; and the acyclic orientation for t = 18 (n = 11t <= 198). Any such witness would give m(4) <= 11t < 209.

rows.title: Pent5(D25) is 4-dichromatic (would give m(4) <= 125) — disproved, chi_vec = 3
rows.statement: The digraph Pent5(D25) — 5 disjoint copies of D25 indexed by i in Z_5, where D25 = C5<-5 is the oriented triangle-free digraph on vertices u_{p,j} (p in Z_5 = pack/cycle position, j in {0..4} = column) with arcs u_{p,j}->u_{p+1,j'} for all j != j' and u_{p+1,j}->u_{p,j}; all internal D25 arcs kept, plus for each copy i, each pack p, and each column j the interface arc (i,p,j) -> (i+1 mod 5, p, j+1 mod 5) (n = 125, 750 arcs; oracle-verified oriented and triangle-free) — has dichromatic number chi_vec >= 4; this would give m(4) <= 125, where m(k) is the least order of an oriented triangle-free digraph with chi_vec >= k (paper benchmark: m(4) <= 209).

rows.title: Cyclic directed Mycielskian M_r(D25) reaches chi_vec >= 4 — disproved for r in {5,7} (and cone cases r=1,2,3)
rows.statement: There exists r in {5,7} such that the cyclic generalized directed Mycielskian M_r(D25) — vertex set Z_r x V(D25) where D25 = C5<-5 (vertices u_{p,j}, p in Z_5, j in {0..4}, arcs u_{p,j}->u_{p+1,j'} for j != j', u_{p+1,j}->u_{p,j}); layer 0 induces a copy of D25, layers 1..r-1 are independent sets, and for every arc u->v of D25 and every layer t in Z_r there is a cross arc (t,u) -> (t+1 mod r, v) — is an oriented triangle-free digraph on N = 25r vertices with dichromatic number chi_vec >= 4 (giving m(4) <= 125 or 175).

rows.title: Bounded-degree cyclic-shift substitution interface S_{ell,d}(D25) amplifies to chi_vec >= 4 — disproved exhaustively for d in {2,3}, ell in {3,5,7}
rows.statement: There exist ell in {3,5,7}, d in {2,3}, and a set O of d distinct cyclic-shift offsets such that S_{ell,d}(D25) — ell disjoint copies of D25 = C5<-5 (defined as in the D25 rows) on a directed ell-cycle, where consecutive copies k, k+1 are joined by the d-regular forward interface consisting, for each offset o in O, of the perfect-matching arcs from vertex x of copy k to vertex x+o (mod 25, in the coded vertex numbering) of copy k+1 (generator scripts/subst_interface_d.py) — is a triangle-free oriented digraph on n = 25*ell <= 175 vertices with dichromatic number chi_vec >= 4.

rows.title: Oriented triangle-free abelian Cayley digraph with chi_vec >= 4 at |S| <= 4 — disproved by exhaustive sweep
rows.statement: There exist an abelian group G in the searched list (Z3xZ3, Z3xZ5, Z5xZ3, Z3xZ7, Z7xZ3, Z3^3, Z9xZ3, Z5xZ5, and the other groups enumerated in scripts/cayley_seed_search.py) and a half-system S subset of G (0 not in S, S and -S disjoint — this makes the Cayley digraph an oriented graph) with |S| <= 4 such that Cay(G,S) (vertex set G, arcs g -> g+s for all g in G, s in S) is triangle-free and has dichromatic number chi_vec >= 4.

rows.title: Spare-capacity gadget C7<-7 substitution reaches chi_vec >= 4 — disproved (n = 245, 343, and edge-maximalized variant)
rows.statement: At least one of the following explicit digraphs has dichromatic number chi_vec >= 4: (i) the odd-directed-cycle complete-pack substitution of C7<-7 with backbone length 5 (n = 245); (ii) the same with backbone length 7 (n = 343); (iii) C7<-7 after greedily adding all 89 addable spare arcs to edge-maximality — where C7<-7 is the 7-backward-blowup of the directed 7-cycle (vertices u_{p,j}, p in Z_7, j in {0..6}, arcs u_{p,j}->u_{p+1,j'} for j != j' and u_{p+1,j}->u_{p,j}; n = 49, oriented, triangle-free, chi_vec = 3, non-edge-maximal with 343 addable non-edges), and the substitution places copies of C7<-7 on a directed odd cycle joined by dense complete-pack forward interfaces as coded for the G9 run.

rows.title: Process-graph orientation route: a_vec(n) = O(sqrt(n log n)) via the triangle-free process
rows.statement: There is a constant C > 0 such that, with probability tending to 1 as n -> infinity, the graph G_n produced by the triangle-free random graph process on n vertices (start from the empty graph on [n]; repeatedly add an edge chosen uniformly at random among the non-edges whose addition creates no triangle, until the graph is maximally triangle-free; Bohman-Keevash) admits an orientation D (each edge {u,v} receives exactly one of the arcs u->v, v->u, so D is an oriented graph: a digraph with no loops and no pair of opposite arcs) in which every acyclic set has size at most C*sqrt(n*log n), where an acyclic set of D is a set S of vertices such that the induced subdigraph D[S] contains no directed cycle. This implies the open upper half of the paper's Conjecture 3: a_vec(n) = O(sqrt(n log n)), where a_vec(n) is the minimum over all n-vertex triangle-free oriented graphs D of alpha_vec(D) = the maximum size of an acyclic set of D.
rows.notes: Verified distinct from parent Conjecture 3 (pins the specific graph family).

rows.title: FPY 2024 bound pi(L_fpy box L_fpy, r) <= 96 for all roots — published but locally uncertified (the folder's blocked reproduction gate)
rows.statement: Let L_fpy be the simple graph on vertex set {0,...,7} with the 13 edges {0-1, 0-2, 1-3, 2-4, 2-5, 2-6, 3-4, 3-5, 3-6, 3-7, 4-7, 5-7, 6-7}, and let L_fpy box L_fpy be the Cartesian product (vertex set V(L_fpy) x V(L_fpy); (u,v) ~ (u',v') iff (u = u' and vv' in E(L_fpy)) or (v = v' and uu' in E(L_fpy))). A configuration is C : V -> Z_{>=0} with size |C| = sum_v C(v); a pebbling move removes 2 pebbles from a vertex u with C(u) >= 2 and adds 1 pebble to a neighbour of u; C is r-solvable iff some finite sequence of pebbling moves yields at least one pebble on r. The rooted pebbling number pi(G, r) is the least t such that every configuration of size t on G is r-solvable, and pi(G) = max_{r in V(G)} pi(G, r). PROPOSITION: for every root r = (r1, r2) in V(L_fpy) x V(L_fpy), pi(L_fpy box L_fpy, r) <= 96; equivalently pi(L_fpy box L_fpy) <= 96.

rows.title: Lemke-square instance of Graham's pebbling conjecture: pi(L_fpy box L_fpy) <= 64 (class-0 dichotomy)
rows.statement: Let L_fpy be the simple graph on vertex set {0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7} with the 13 edges {0-1, 0-2, 1-3, 2-4, 2-5, 2-6, 3-4, 3-5, 3-6, 3-7, 4-7, 5-7, 6-7} (the 8-vertex Lemke graph in the Flocco-Pulaj-Yerger labelling; degree sequence (5,4,4,3,3,3,2,2)). Let L_fpy box L_fpy denote the Cartesian product: vertex set V(L_fpy) x V(L_fpy), with (u,v) adjacent to (u',v') iff either (u = u' and vv' in E(L_fpy)) or (v = v' and uu' in E(L_fpy)). A configuration on a graph G is a function C : V(G) -> Z_{>=0}; its size is |C| = sum_v C(v). A pebbling move from C removes 2 pebbles from a vertex u with C(u) >= 2 and adds 1 pebble to a neighbour v of u. C is r-solvable iff some finite sequence of pebbling moves starting from C produces a configuration with at least one pebble on the root r; otherwise C is r-unsolvable. The pebbling number pi(G) is the least t such that for every r in V(G), every configuration of size t on G is r-solvable. CONJECTURE: pi(L_fpy box L_fpy) <= 64. Equivalently (since pi(G) >= |V(G)| for every graph G: place one pebble on each vertex other than r, then no move is possible), pi(L_fpy box L_fpy) = 64, i.e. L_fpy box L_fpy is class 0. Equivalently: for every r in V(L_fpy) x V(L_fpy), every configuration of size 64 on L_fpy box L_fpy is r-solvable. A refutation would be an explicit finite certificate: a root r and a 64-pebble r-unsolvable configuration.

rows.title: Naive active-bag DP signature is extension-complete (refuted)
rows.statement: Let T be a tournament with radius-2 score windows I_v = [max(0, d^-(v)-2), min(n-1, d^-(v)+2)] (d^-(v) = indegree), satisfying Hall feasibility (for every position interval [l,r], #{v : I_v subseteq [l,r]} <= r-l+1); every LFO places each v at a position in I_v (proved score-window lemma). For a valid prefix (an initial segment of an order extendable in principle to an LFO, with forced back-arcs preloaded by the forced/flexible solver) at cut p, define the ACTIVE-BAG SIGNATURE as: the set of active vertices (v with p in I_v), the placed subset of the active bag, the loaded back-arc degrees of the active vertices, and the component partition of the loaded back-arc graph restricted to the active bag. CLAIM (refuted): any two valid same-length prefixes of the same T with identical active-bag signature have the same extendability - both extend to a complete LFO or neither does.

rows.title: Strong AC-completeness of the apex-cut CSP (refuted by witness 8#1079)
rows.statement: Let T be a tournament. For each vertex v define the apex-cut graph C_v: an edge ab for each directed triangle v->a->b->v (a in N+(v), b in N-(v), a->b). Domain D_v = { P subseteq V\{v} : |P| <= 2 and C_v - P is a linear forest } (P = candidate set of F-neighbours of v, since a Path-FAS witness F must be a linear forest, giving |N_F(v)| <= 2; the proved apex-cut lemma says F hits every directed triangle iff E(C_v - P_v) subseteq F for all v). Run arc-consistency (AC-3) on the binary constraints (symmetry: u in P_v iff v in P_u; forced edges: xy in E(C_v - P_v) implies y in P_x and x in P_y). Let the mandatory-forced graph M be the set of edges forced into F by every AC-surviving candidate of some vertex. CLAIM (refuted): if after AC every domain D_v is nonempty and M is acyclic as an undirected graph, then T is Path-FAS YES, i.e. some order of T has a linear-forest back-arc graph.

rows.title: D80 non-adjacent flip hypothesis: iso-11 always forces a realizable mixed port vector
rows.statement: With the definitions of row lemma-C: call an LFO sigma of a 2-port gadget iso-11 if it realizes the both-back-arc equality value with both port arcs isolated, i.e. each port's tournament arc is a back-arc of sigma forming an isolated K_2 component of B_sigma (all four port endpoints have B_sigma-degree exactly 1, their only back-arc being their own port arc). CLAIM (refuted): for every tournament T with two vertex-disjoint ports, if T admits an iso-11 LFO then T also admits an LFO realizing a mixed port vector ((0,1) or (1,0)); equivalently, no iso-11 gadget has R contained in {(0,0),(1,1)}.

rows.title: Dormant-Matching Quotient Lemma (multiset aggregate) (refuted)
rows.statement: Setting of row visible-latent. Let H be the forced-backedge digraph of T (arcs u->v of T whose score windows are disjoint with I_v strictly below I_u, so every candidate LFO places v before u and u->v is a back-arc; H-edges are preloaded). At cut p, a DORMANT H-COMPONENT is a connected component C of the underlying undirected graph of H with C disjoint from the active band A_p and containing at least one closed (window ended, placed) and one future (window not yet open) vertex. Its LOCAL PROFILE is (canonical shape of C = sorted tuple of H-degrees; sorted list over v in C of (status(v) in {closed, future}, loaded back-arc degree of v, sorted set of active-band position indices that v can hit via a flexible back-arc)). The DORMANT AGGREGATE of a prefix is the multiset of local profiles of its dormant H-components. CLAIM (refuted): any two valid length-p prefixes of the same T with the same visible-latent signature and equal dormant aggregates have the same extendability to a complete LFO.

rows.title: Double-cyclic-triangle hub forces LFO NO (refuted)
rows.statement: CLAIM (refuted): for every tournament T, if some vertex v satisfies |N+(v)| = |N-(v)| = 3 and both induced subtournaments T[N+(v)] and T[N-(v)] are directed 3-cycles (cyclic triangles), then T admits no LFO, i.e. no order of T has a back-arc graph that is a linear forest (undirected max degree <= 2 and acyclic).

rows.title: Capacity-form Lemma C (Fanout Barrier): no EQ_2 gadget has joint capacity on both equality values
rows.statement: Let T be a tournament (an orientation of the complete graph) on vertex set V, n = |V|. For a linear order sigma of V, an arc u->v of T is a back-arc of sigma iff sigma places v before u; the back-arc graph B_sigma is the undirected graph on V with an edge {u,v} for every back-arc u->v. A linear forest is an undirected graph with maximum degree <= 2 and no cycle. sigma is an LFO iff B_sigma is a linear forest. A port is an ordered pair (x,y) of distinct vertices; its raw bit under sigma is b = 1 iff y precedes x in sigma. Fix two vertex-disjoint ports P1 = (x1,y1), P2 = (x2,y2) (the four endpoints pairwise distinct) and an orientation o = (o1,o2) in {0,1}^2; the realized relation is R = { (b1(sigma) XOR o1, b2(sigma) XOR o2) : sigma an LFO of T }. The gadget is EQ_2 iff R = {(0,0),(1,1)}. A vector (c1,c2) has JOINT CAPACITY iff some LFO sigma realizing it has all four port endpoints x1,y1,x2,y2 of B_sigma-degree <= 1. CONJECTURE: for every n, every tournament T, every vertex-disjoint port pair and orientation with R = EQ_2, joint capacity does NOT hold on both (0,0) and (1,1). Equivalently (via the in-folder proved theorem cap-11 = iso-11): no EQ_2 gadget is simultaneously cap-00 and iso-11.

rows.title: One-value Lemma C (D76): joint capacity on a single equality value forces R != EQ_k
rows.statement: With the definitions of row lemma-C generalized to k >= 2 vertex-disjoint ports (2k distinct endpoints, orientation o in {0,1}^k, realized relation R over all LFOs, EQ_k = {0^k, 1^k}, and joint capacity of a vector = some LFO realizing it with all 2k port endpoints of back-arc-graph degree <= 1): CLAIM (refuted): for every k >= 2 and every k-port tournament gadget, if some equality vector v in {0^k, 1^k} has joint port capacity, then R != EQ_k.

rows.title: Visible-latent extension-equivalence (refuted)
rows.statement: Setting of row active-bag-dp (tournament with Hall-feasible radius-2 score windows, forced/flexible solver states). At cut i let A_i be the active band (vertices whose score window contains i) and O_i the old-prefix ports (already-placed vertices that some unplaced active vertex can still hit with a flexible back-arc). The VISIBLE-LATENT SIGNATURE of a valid pruned prefix records: (a) the identities and placed status of A_i; (b) the loaded back-arc degrees of all vertices in A_i; (c) the old-port incidences from unplaced active vertices into O_i; (d) the component partition of the loaded back-arc graph induced on A_i union O_i, with old vertices anonymized except through their port roles. CLAIM (refuted): any two valid pruned prefixes of the same T with the same prefix SET and the same visible-latent signature have the same extendability to a complete LFO.

rows.title: Joint-invariant ansatz condition (a): I(v*) >= T at the max-degsum ear
rows.statement: Let G be a 2-tree on n >= 4 vertices, v a simplicial degree-2 ear with supporting edge {a,b}, H := G - v, and w := e_a + e_b in R^{n-1} (so ||w||^2 = 2). Let P- be the spectral projector of A(H) onto the span of eigenvectors with negative eigenvalue, and define W-(v) := ||P- w||^2, M1-(v) := w^T A(H) P- w, M2-(v) := w^T A(H)^2 P- w, and I(v) := W-(v) + (M1-(v))^2 / M2-(v) if M2-(v) > 0, else I(v) := W-(v). Conjecture (condition (a), with T := 0.4122 = 2061/5000): for every 2-tree G on n >= 4 vertices there exists a simplicial degree-2 ear v* attaining the maximum degsum (as in row maxdegsum-selector) with I(v*) >= T.

rows.title: Joint-invariant ansatz condition (b) / slot-shift wall O12.2
rows.statement: With I and T = 0.4122 defined as in row ansatz-a and delta- as in row lprime: for every 2-tree G on n >= 4 vertices and every simplicial degree-2 ear v of G, if I(v) >= T then delta-(v) is in [17/16, 47/16].

rows.title: b.minor: delta-(v*) >= 1 at the max-degsum ear
rows.statement: With definitions as in rows lprime and maxdegsum-selector: for every 2-tree G on n >= 4 vertices there exists a simplicial degree-2 ear v* attaining the maximum degsum with delta-(v*) >= 1.

rows.title: AKMPZ Conjecture 9.2 restricted to 2-trees (strict square-energy gaps)
rows.statement: With s+, s- and 2-trees as in row lprime (K_3-based recursive definition): for every 2-tree G on n >= 4 vertices, s+(G) > n - 1 and s-(G) > n - 1. (For n >= 4 a 2-tree has 2n-3 edges, so it is neither a tree nor a complete graph; hence this is exactly the restriction of Akbari–Kumar–Mohar–Pragada–Zhang Conjecture 9.2, arXiv:2506.07264, to the 2-tree class.)
rows.notes: Verified distinct from parent axv_2506_07264_02 (proper restriction to the 2-tree class; the full Conjecture 9.2 is the existing corpus row).

rows.title: Fan family floor: delta-(F_n) >= (7 - sqrt(17))/2 for all n >= 4
rows.statement: Let F_n := K_1 v P_{n-1} (the fan on n vertices: a hub vertex adjacent to all n-1 vertices of a path, plus the path edges); F_n is a 2-tree, its simplicial degree-2 ears are exactly the two path endpoints, and F_n minus an endpoint is isomorphic to F_{n-1}. Define delta-(F_n) := s-(F_n) - s-(F_{n-1}) with s- as in row lprime. Claim: for every integer n >= 4, delta-(F_n) >= (7 - sqrt(17))/2 (approximately 1.43845, hence > 17/16); equivalently the minimum over n >= 4 is attained at n = 4 where F_4 = B_2 = K_4 - e gives equality.

rows.title: (L') existential ear-selection lemma for 2-trees
rows.statement: Definitions: for a finite simple graph G with adjacency matrix A(G), s+(G) (resp. s-(G)) is the sum of the squares of the positive (resp. negative) eigenvalues of A(G). A 2-tree is defined recursively: K_3 is a 2-tree, and any graph obtained from a 2-tree by adding one new vertex adjacent to exactly the two endpoints of an existing edge is a 2-tree.
